commandsRun.observation: Passed; the trusted disconnected state auto-retried and recovered back to the paired UI without manual retry or a new pairing flow.

assertions.reason: The reconnect/catch-up user surface passed in both integration and widget harnesses: disconnect left the thread readable, exposed reconnect controls, and restored missed items without duplicating the already streamed live item.

Partial reconnect evidence passed for preserved trust/no re-pairing and restored selected thread context, but the available reconnect/offline user-surface harnesses did not exercise pending approvals restoration or a full auth/subscription/repo-context restore bundle together, so the complete assertion could not be concluded honestly from user-surface evidence alone.

Offline and disconnected states were explicit across the tested surfaces: cached thread views showed clear offline messaging, mutating actions were blocked, and the trusted pairing surface preserved trust while surfacing Bridge disconnected instead of silently queuing control actions.

Previously synced thread data remained readable offline in both list and detail flows, and the relaunch integration restored the cached selected thread with visible offline/stale messaging while keeping mutating actions disabled.

frictions: The flutter-mobile-widget-tests and flutter-mobile-integration-tests surfaces provide assertion-backed Flutter command logs rather than standalone screenshots, videos, or websocket captures, so this report relies on saved test logs for user-surface evidence.
Each targeted integration assertion rebuilt and reinstalled the Android debug app before execution, adding roughly 50-55 seconds of harness startup time per run.

blockers.description: No targeted reconnect/offline Flutter harness exercised pending approvals restoration or a combined reconnect flow proving auth, subscriptions, active thread, approvals, and repo context together.
